text.title: Response Caching for LLM Cost Reduction
text.summary: ResponseCache design using SHA-256 content hash keys, TTL strategy by content volatility, Redis vs. in-memory backends, and cache invalidation rules.

rule.statement: Cache only deterministic outputs: set temperature=0 as a precondition before writing to cache; never cache outputs from temperature > 0.
rule.rationale: Non-deterministic outputs at temperature > 0 produce different correct answers for the same input; caching them returns stale stochastic results.

rule.statement: Use SHA-256 of (model + messages + relevant kwargs) as the cache key, not the raw prompt string.
rule.rationale: Including model in the key prevents cross-model cache hits. Including max_tokens prevents returning truncated cached responses for requests with higher limits.

example.note: sort_keys=True ensures dict ordering does not affect the hash.

rule.statement: Set TTL relative to content volatility: static documentation → 7 days; user queries → 1-4 hours; real-time data summaries → do not cache.
rule.rationale: A 7-day TTL on a prompt that references live prices serves stale data. A 1-hour TTL on FAQ answers misses most of the cache savings opportunity.

rule.statement: Invalidate cache entries when the model version changes — Redis TTL alone does not handle this; add model version to the cache key or flush on version deploy.
rule.rationale: Responses cached under model vX may be stylistically or factually different from vX+1; serving old responses after a model update degrades quality silently.

rule.statement: Track cache hit rate per endpoint; if hit rate is below 20%, the cache key is too specific or TTL is too short.
rule.rationale: Below 20% hit rate the cache adds latency overhead without meaningful cost savings; diagnose key specificity or TTL before scaling.

antipattern.description: Caching inside an agentic loop using session-scoped keys.
antipattern.reason: Agents in a loop must observe state changes between turns; session-scoped cache keys return stale tool-call results and cause the agent to act on outdated information.
